excerpt: The Hoover article states that China began the war on 17 February 1979 and ceased fire on 16 March 1979.
transform_note: Used to bound the rerun strictly to the formal invasion window.
confidence_note: High confidence on the invasion window.

excerpt: Path summarizes the Chinese operation as involving at least 450,000 troops overall, but about 80,000 actually committed to the invasion fighting.
transform_note: Only the actually committed figure is used for theater exposure; larger nationwide totals are excluded.
confidence_note: Best scholarly theater-force anchor recovered in this rerun.

excerpt: Path reports that Chinese forces were met by roughly 75,000 to 100,000 Vietnamese militia and troops.
transform_note: Used as the defender-side exposure range.
confidence_note: Reasonable frontier-theater defender range, but still a synthesis estimate.

excerpt: HistoryNet characterizes the Vietnamese regular combat force on the main fronts as about 50,000, distinct from broader militia and border-guard totals.
transform_note: Used to justify keeping the best combatant exposure estimate in the committed-frontier range rather than using national totals.
confidence_note: Contextual support, not the main denominator driver.

excerpt: Path summarizes the invasion as causing about 25,000 to 28,000 Chinese dead.
transform_note: Used as one side of the combined military direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Substantial uncertainty remains, but it is the tightest scholarly range recovered here.

excerpt: Path gives an estimate of about 20,000 Vietnamese military dead in the invasion.
transform_note: Used as the other side of the combined military direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Likely approximate, but recoverable as a direct combatant-death anchor.

excerpt: The Hoover article says each side suffered roughly 30,000 deaths, while also acknowledging the numbers are unverified.
transform_note: Used only as an outer high-end plausibility check for the combined military death estimate.
confidence_note: Lower confidence than Path and not used as the midpoint.

geographic_scope: Tong Chup village / pig farm site
excerpt: VnExpress recounts that Chinese troops killed 43 civilians at Tong Chup during the border war.
transform_note: One of three corroborating claims for the Tong Chup micro-episode.

geographic_scope: Tong Chup village / pig farm site
excerpt: Vietnam News reports that 43 women and children at a local farm were killed during the 1979 invasion.
transform_note: Second corroborating claim for the Tong Chup micro-episode.
confidence_note: Good corroboration, though still retrospective and not independently verified.

geographic_scope: Tong Chup village, Hưng Đạo commune, Cao Bằng
excerpt: VietBao states that Chinese soldiers attacked a pig farm in Tong Chup on 9 March 1979, killing 43 people and dumping the bodies into a well.
pages_or_sections: article body, lines around 46 and location passage
transform_note: Third corroborating claim and location/date anchor for Tong Chup.
confidence_note: Strong enough for a one-day civilian-death count, but still not independently verified outside Vietnamese reporting.
